situation.headline: Active US military strikes on Iranian territory combined with Strait of Hormuz seizure operations signal the most significant direct US-Iran kinetic engagement in decades, with cascading risks across energy, regional stability, and nuclear diplomacy simultaneously in motion

domains.name: Middle East
domains.label: regional escalation 90d
domains.status: US strikes on Omidiyeh Airport and coastal defense infrastructure, Iranian refusal of IAEA inspections, Houthi resumption of Red Sea harassment, Saudi-Houthi ceasefire collapse, and explosions at Bandar Abbas and Qeshm Island constitute a multi-front kinetic escalation ladder with no visible off-ramp in the next 90 days.

domains.name: Indo-Pacific
domains.label: China coercion escalation
domains.status: A 14-nation joint reaffirmation of the 2016 South China Sea tribunal ruling, including Japan, has provoked sharp Chinese diplomatic protests and summoning of ambassadors, hardening an already tense legal-military standoff as Beijing watches US attention consumed by Iran.

domains.name: Europe
domains.label: NATO cohesion fracture
domains.status: Russian FSB attribution for multi-year cyberattacks on EU government networks across ten member states reinforces alliance solidarity against Moscow, while Ukraine's sustained Sea of Azov drone campaign against 105 Russian vessels over eight days demonstrates continued offensive capacity, keeping European resolve relatively firm.

domains.name: Energy
domains.label: supply shock 60d
domains.status: US military operations degrading Iranian coastal missile and radar sites along the Strait of Hormuz, combined with Houthi resumption of Red Sea small-boat harassment and tanker incidents near Aden, place approximately 20 percent of global oil transit under active military management, with gas power costs already at a 17-year high and Strategic Reserve draws straining aging infrastructure.

domains.name: US Politics
domains.label: House flip Nov 2026
domains.status: Death of Senator Graham triggers a South Carolina vacancy race, DOJ subpoenas against NYT journalists and FBI leaker hunts signal a sharpening executive-press confrontation, Trump sons' defense tech investments raise ethics exposure, and consumer debt stress from food costs creates economic headwinds; Kalshi Democratic Sweep contract sits at 49 percent with minimal 24h movement, suggesting markets see the race as genuinely open.

situation.unseen: The simultaneous collapse of the US-Iran MOU, Iran's refusal of IAEA access, US seizure of Hormuz coordination, and Ukraine's destruction of 105 Russian vessels in the Sea of Azov are converging in a single week in ways that will force China to recalibrate its own Taiwan timeline calculus: Beijing is watching the US demonstrate both willingness and capacity for sustained kinetic operations against a nuclear-adjacent adversary's maritime infrastructure while simultaneously managing a European theater, which is a direct data point Beijing's PLA planners are feeding into their cross-strait risk models, making the South China Sea diplomatic flare-up a potential feint or pressure test rather than an end in itself.
situation.criticalWindow: The next 30 days of Iranian decision-making on IAEA access and MOU compliance are the most time-sensitive hinge point: if Tehran formally abandons the deal while US forces hold Hormuz coordination posture, the diplomatic ladder collapses entirely and allied re-insurance of Strait transit becomes a de facto naval blockade requiring sustained force projection that will stress US carrier availability and invite Chinese opportunism in the Indo-Pacific simultaneously.
watchFor: Iranian declaration of full MOU withdrawal or announcement of uranium enrichment acceleration above current levels, which would trigger Israeli and Gulf state unilateral action planning independent of Washington

Houthi or Iranian proxy strike successfully disabling a major oil tanker transiting Hormuz under US coordination, testing the credibility of the US protection umbrella and potentially spiking Brent by 15-25 percent in hours

Chinese naval or coast guard action against a Philippine or Japanese vessel in the South China Sea timed to exploit US Hormuz distraction, converting the current diplomatic flare-up into a kinetic incident

South Carolina Senate vacancy appointment of Scott Bessent removing him from Treasury at a moment of peak energy-market and tariff stress, creating a leadership vacuum in the one cabinet position most critical to managing a simultaneous oil shock and potential recession the market already prices at 43 percent probability by 2027
situation.confidenceNote: Key uncertainties include whether the US strikes on Iranian territory represent a defined campaign with explicit termination criteria or open-ended attrition, the actual operational status of Iranian nuclear facilities post-strike, whether the Graham death report is confirmed and the political succession timeline in South Carolina, the degree to which the US-Iran MOU had meaningful compliance history before this crisis phase, and the reliability of Sea of Azov vessel-destruction counts from Ukrainian OSINT sources who have strong incentive to inflate figures.
situation.topPattern: The thread connecting all major theaters is that US hard-power credibility is being simultaneously tested and demonstrated in the Strait of Hormuz, creating a brief window of deterrence leverage over China in the South China Sea that the 14-nation tribunal reaffirmation is designed to exploit, but this window closes rapidly if Iran successfully imposes costs on Hormuz transit or if domestic political turbulence from Graham's death, DOJ press conflicts, and consumer economic stress constrains the administration's freedom of action; Kalshi markets pricing Marco Rubio VP at 24 percent down 3 cents and JD Vance presidential nomination at 42 percent up 1 cent suggest subtle repositioning around succession risk that sophisticated money is beginning to price.

signals.headline: Lindsey Graham reported dead from brief illness after Ukraine return
signals.summary: Drudge headlines report Senator Lindsey Graham dead from sudden illness following recent Ukraine visit. McConnell clarifies he was hospitalized last month after a fall, suggesting possible confusion or misreporting. Senate faces renewed focus on aging membership vulnerabilities.
signals.signal: If Graham death confirmed, immediate Senate seat vacancy triggers succession crisis during active Iran-US military escalation and Ukraine aid debates. Treasury Secretary Bessent replacement option signals administration focus on economic continuity over Senate consensus-building.
signals.contradicts: McConnell clarification suggests Graham may not be deceased; headline may be speculative or false alarm. Requires verification before strategic assessment.

signals.headline: US strikes Iranian targets; Iran retaliates; Saudi-Houthi ceasefire collapses
signals.summary: US conducted strikes on Iranian air-defense systems, coastal radars, and drone capabilities on July 12 to degrade Strait of Hormuz threat. Iran's Foreign Ministry rejected IAEA inspections and vowed to abandon MOU if US non-compliance continues. Saudi strikes on Sana'a airport broke Yemen ceasefire; Iranian Mahan Air delegation landed at Hodeidah under protection.
signals.signal: Escalation spiral underway: US-Iran direct military action, collapse of Saudi-Houthi de-escalation track, Iranian defiance on nuclear transparency. Strait transits resuming under US coordination (20 vessels) but at elevated risk. Regional proxy wars re-igniting simultaneously.
signals.contradicts: Trump's messaging on Iran containment contradicts rapid military escalation and Iranian resolve to sustain regional operations despite strikes.

signals.headline: 14 nations affirm 2016 South China Sea tribunal ruling; China rejects and summons Japan envoy
signals.summary: US, UK, Japan, and 11 other nations jointly reaffirmed the 2016 arbitral tribunal invalidating Chinese maritime claims in South China Sea. China lodged formal protests and summoned Japanese envoy in response. Joint statement emphasizes free and open Indo-Pacific commitment.
signals.signal: Coordinated alliance messaging on rules-based order directly challenges Beijing's nine-dash-line claims and signals unified response to coercive maritime actions. Japan's explicit participation escalates stakes for regional stability and Taiwan contingency planning.
signals.contradicts: China's defiant posturing and refusal to recognize tribunal authority suggests continued willingness to escalate maritime provocations despite multilateral pressure.

signals.headline: DOJ subpoenas NYT journalists; Trump administration escalates media pressure; FBI hunts leakers
signals.summary: Drudge reports DOJ subpoenaing New York Times journalists and Trump administration applying pressure on media outlets while FBI investigates leakers. Context suggests coordinated effort to suppress adverse reporting.
signals.signal: Press freedom degradation underway at federal level during active military escalations (Iran, Ukraine). Media control attempt during crisis period raises constitutional and intelligence-sharing risks with allied governments.
signals.contradicts: None; aligns with Trump's public rhetoric on media as enemy and previous attempts to restrict classified information access.

signals.headline: Strategic Petroleum Reserve drawdowns push 17-year-old system to breaking point
signals.summary: Frequent oil draws from Strategic Petroleum Reserve are stressing aging infrastructure. Gas power costs hit 17-year highs and continue climbing amid energy demands.
signals.signal: US energy reserves depletion during Middle East escalation and Ukraine aid reduces buffer against further Iran Strait disruptions. Domestic energy cost spikes erode economic stability and may constrain administration's ability to sustain dual military/aid operations.
signals.contradicts: None; consistent with energy supply fragility warnings from prior quarters.

signals.headline: Russia FSB confirmed behind years of EU cyberattacks on critical infrastructure
signals.summary: EU officially announced Russia's FSB responsible for sustained cyberattacks on governmental networks and critical infrastructure across 10 EU member states including France, Germany, Poland, Ukraine. Espionage and sabotage operations span multiple years.
signals.signal: Active Russian cyber warfare on NATO/EU infrastructure during peak US commitment to Ukraine suggests Moscow escalation strategy independent of US-Iran military action. EU vulnerability to supply-chain and energy sabotage during current crisis increases transatlantic coordination burden.
signals.contradicts: None; reinforces known threat pattern but formal EU attribution signals political decision to highlight attribution publicly.

signals.headline: China and Russia intelligence sites in Cuba flagged as national security threat
signals.summary: Fox News reports Chinese and Russian intelligence operations in Cuba designated as national security threat to US. Timing coincides with multi-theater escalation.
signals.signal: Western Hemisphere intelligence infrastructure allows China-Russia coordination on US adversary operations. Cuba positioning suggests preparation for contingency operations during prolonged US Middle East engagement or Taiwan crisis.
signals.contradicts: None; aligns with strategic competition warnings.

scanNote: Signal quality degraded by Lindsey Graham death headline requiring urgent verification; remaining Middle East, Indo-Pacific, and cyber signals align with authentic geopolitical escalation patterns observed across multiple independent sources.

markets.label: Trump Bear Case 2026
markets.rationale: Active military engagement with Iran, DOJ subpoenas of journalists, economic stress signals (debt, food costs), and Supreme Court public fractures all elevate tail-risk scenarios, but markets priced this down 2 cents suggesting traders see the administration weathering current crises.

markets.label: Recession in 2027
markets.rationale: Millions draining savings to afford food, 17-year high gas power costs, frequent SPR draws pushing the reserve system toward failure, and an AI bubble concern over a $4 trillion concentration all point to elevated recession probability above the current 43% market price.

markets.label: Vance 2028 GOP Nominee
markets.rationale: Graham's death reshuffles Senate dynamics and creates a succession vacuum, but the Iran conflict and domestic economic strain could either entrench or damage Vance's positioning depending on outcome; Rubio's simultaneous 3-cent VP drop suggests some ticket reshuffling speculation is live.

markets.label: Rubio 2028 GOP Nominee
markets.rationale: Rubio is deeply associated with the Iran policy and Strait of Hormuz operation as Secretary of State; a successful outcome boosts him, but his VP market dropped 3 cents today suggesting markets are modestly de-risking his overall 2028 positioning simultaneously.

markets.label: AOC Runs for 2028 Dem Nomination
markets.rationale: The 6-cent spike to 59% is the sharpest single-day move in the entire prediction-market dataset and likely reflects a concrete signal — possibly a public statement or filing activity — making this the highest-confidence directional signal in the feed; STRATWATCH marks it slightly above market given the momentum.

markets.label: AOC 2028 Dem Nominee
markets.rationale: The run-market spike (59%) translates into only a moderate nominee probability given a crowded field; Ossoff's 1-cent rise and Newsom at 20% nominee odds indicate the field remains genuinely competitive even if AOC enters.

markets.label: Trump Buys Greenland Before 2027
markets.rationale: With US military and diplomatic bandwidth fully consumed by active Iran operations, Yemen maritime incidents, Ukraine drone campaign support, and domestic political crises, executive attention required to advance Greenland acquisition is effectively unavailable through end of 2026.

markets.label: Todd Blanche Next AG
markets.rationale: Blanche holds 71% market price with a 2-cent rise today; the DOJ subpoenaing NYT journalists and FBI hunting leakers signals an AG aligned with aggressive press and leak prosecution is already functionally in place, supporting continuity of current leadership rather than replacement.

markets.label: Venezuela as 51st State Before 2029
markets.rationale: Cuba offering Trump a luxury island to avoid invasion signals regional actors assess US intervention appetite as real but targeted, while Venezuela annexation as a formal state faces constitutional, military, and diplomatic barriers that remain insurmountable in this time horizon regardless of rhetorical escalation.

watchSignals: Senate seat succession after Graham's death - Treasury Secretary Bessent or other Trump ally appointment will signal administration control of upper chamber and policy direction
Iran escalation trajectory - US strikes on Iranian targets, IRGC response timing, and Strait of Hormuz transit security indicate whether regional conflict enters sustained hot phase or stabilizes
Ukraine Sea of Azov shipping collapse - 105 vessels hit in 8 days suggests capability to degrade Russian logistics; watch for Russian counter-response and NATO involvement escalation
South China Sea coalition fracture - China's rejection of 14-nation support for 2016 ruling; monitor whether additional nations defect or hold line on freedom of navigation
Democratic 2028 nominee crystallization - AOC trending 59% on prediction markets while establishment figures (Newsom 20%, Beshear 3%) fragment; watch primary signals and party unity
Middle East US military posture - Watch for Iranian retaliation scope, Saudi-Houthi ceasefire status, and whether US coordinates Strait of Hormuz escort operations indicating sustained commitment

groundTruth.headline: Senator Lindsey Graham dead after brief illness following Ukraine return
groundTruth.summary: South Carolina Republican senator and Trump ally Lindsey Graham died unexpectedly after returning from Ukraine visit. Succession triggers immediate power dynamic shift in Senate. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent is leading candidate for replacement, signaling Trump's ability to consolidate executive-legislative control.
groundTruth.signal: Removes senior Trump critic-turned-ally from Senate; Bessent appointment would signal prioritization of economic policy continuity over legislative independence.

groundTruth.headline: US executes major strike campaign against Iranian military targets; Strait of Hormuz under active US naval coordination
groundTruth.summary: CENTCOM conducted dozens of strikes on Iranian air defense, radar, missile and drone capabilities on July 12. 20 commercial vessels transited Strait of Hormuz under US coordination. Iran rejected IAEA inspections and threatened to abandon nuclear MOU. Saudi-Houthi ceasefire appears broken.
groundTruth.signal: Indicates Trump administration executing kinetic Iran policy; US reasserting physical control of critical energy chokepoint; regional conflict rapidly moving from diplomatic to military-dominant phase.

groundTruth.headline: Ukrainian drone strikes destroy 105 Russian vessels in Sea of Azov within 8 days; shipping corridor functionally collapsed
groundTruth.summary: Ukrainian forces hit 7 tankers, 5 cargo ships, 2 tugboats and ferry on night of July 13. Cumulative destruction of 105 vessels over 8 days has collapsed Azov/Don/Kerch Russian shipping. Russia's ability to supply occupied territory severely degraded.
groundTruth.signal: Demonstrates Ukraine's sustained offensive capability and force attrition of Russian logistics; indicates conflict entering phase where Russia must choose between accepting territorial loss or major escalation.

groundTruth.headline: 14 nations including Japan, US, UK reaffirm 2016 South China Sea ruling invalidating Chinese maritime claims; China summons envoys and rejects tribunal authority
groundTruth.summary: Coordinated statement from US, UK, Japan and 12 others backing arbitral tribunal decision on South China Sea. China lodged formal protests and rejected ruling as illegal. Indicates hardening coalition on rules-based order vs. Beijing's expansionism.
groundTruth.signal: Alliance cohesion on China containment remains intact; Beijing's isolation on lawfare strategy growing; watch for whether additional nations defect or China escalates coercive responses.

groundTruth.headline: EU publicly attributes multi-year cyberattack campaign against government and critical infrastructure to Russian FSB across 10 member states
groundTruth.summary: European Union announced Russia's Federal Security Service responsible for sustained cyberattacks on France, Germany, Poland, Cyprus, Netherlands, Austria, Slovakia, Romania, Finland, and Ukraine. Targets included governmental networks and critical infrastructure sabotage.
groundTruth.signal: Public attribution signals EU escalating cyber accountability rhetoric; indicates Russian FSB maintaining offensive posture against Europe despite other regional conflicts; NATO cyber deterrence effectiveness questionable.
